what_it_does: This file is the prescriptive, living constitution for the ops module: it defines how the Company’s running system is seen and operated by an AI. It establishes a single console (`company`) and a single registry (`services.json`) as the operational map, uses systemd as the execution muscle, and mandates fail-loud, honest, drift-visible behavior. It also frames the service console as the first instantiation of a general type-view mechanism that should later cover GPU/models, capabilities, jobs/cron, sessions, and cognitive layers without spawning parallel tools.
observations: Declares itself a constitution with frontmatter type/register/module/aliases/tags/relates-to/status.
Built for an environment with no human developers reading files; must be self-describing enough for a cold AI session.
`company` is a symlinked console on PATH that reads `services.json` and shells out to `systemctl`/`journalctl`/socket checks; it owns no state.
`services.json` is the single source of truth for services; adding a service means adding one entry.
systemd units in `ops/systemd/` are canonical; console drives systemd, never replaces it; user linger is on; `company.target` is the boot target.
Honest-over-complete principle: imperfect-but-visible entries are preferred over invisible scatter.
Fail-loud / no-hiding principle: status shows drift and failures; control actions report success/failure explicitly.
Services are the first type-view of a generalized console; later type-views include models/VRAM, capability catalog, jobs/cron timers, sessions, and future cognitive-layers/RHM/modes/data/memory.

Contains dense, dated subsections for GPU resource management, model capability catalog, `ensure_resident` actuator, session fabric, agent-sessions exporter/importer, transcript search, rerank step, and hooks.
Lists many ops files and their roles, plus acceptance tests that act as drift assertions.
standouts: One console + one registry anti-scatter guarantee.
Type-view generalization: same console/registry mechanism instantiated per resource type, not separate tools.
`model_capabilities.json` as file-discovered declared data: add a model by adding one row, no code edit.
`ensure_resident` gated launch/evict actuator with explicit swap-approval ask and no silent eviction.
Session supervisor service is the sole launcher of Claude subprocesses; the console only talks HTTP to it.
conventions: systemd as canonical executor; console as thin driver.
Type-view abstraction instead of one-off tools.
concerns: Extremely long and dense; the volume of detail may undermine the stated goal of being readable cold by a fresh AI.
Many imperative specifics (ports, commands, dates, file paths) embedded in a constitution will stale quickly.
Heavy use of undefined acronyms and labels (RHM, G14, B4, C8.1, F1, D3) without local glossary.
Frequent 'INTERIM' / 'throwaway' / 'not yet instantiated' / 'eventually' language blurs operational vs aspirational status.
Tight coupling to many external files and subsystems not defined here.
notes: A dense operational constitution that tries to be the single cold-start map for AI control of the running system, with services as the first of several planned resource-type views.
questions: What do RHM, G14, B4, C8.1–C8.4, F1, and D3 stand for?
What are the exact schemas of `services.json` and `model_capabilities.json`?
How is the `company` launcher symlinked onto PATH and bootstrapped on a fresh machine?
What is the boundary between this ops constitution and the runtime constitution it relates to?
Which subsystems described are currently implemented, interim, or only planned?
What are the 'Session Fabric filter law' and 'single-writer law'?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be a single self-describing operational control constitution. The file does that, but it also reads like a dense design journal/changelog for many recent additions, so it matches while being much heavier than a concise constitution.
intention_signals: It is trying to create a self-running, self-documenting operational layer that an AI can read once and then operate the whole machine, while preventing duplicate command centers by generalizing one console/registry pattern across every resource type.
novelty: It treats operational control as a constitution file rather than scattered config/scripts, and explicitly designs a generalized 'type-view' console instantiated per resource kind (services, GPU models, capabilities, jobs, sessions) with a single registry truth and fail-loud drift visibility, aimed at AI self-operation.
contribution: A prescriptive, self-describing constitution that consolidates the ops module’s semantics, guarantees, extension rules, and file inventory. It anchors the `company` console/registry pattern and documents how new resource types should fold into the same mechanism rather than become parallel tools.
summary_for_embedding: ops/AGENTS.md is the prescriptive living constitution for the Company's operational control layer, defining a single AI-readable console and registry that drives systemd to see and operate services, GPU models, capabilities, jobs, sessions, and future resource types with fail-loud honesty and no scattered state.
intention_for_embedding: To make the running system operable by a fresh AI session from one self-describing file and registry, preventing invisible scatter and duplicate command centers.
suggested_fields: dependency_edges to runtime constitution and company map
